explanation: The natural language requirement states that the self-test should confirm the oxygen sensor is calibrated. Out of the available atomic propositions, the most directly relevant is 'testOxygenSensorPass' (indicates oxygen sensor test pass (success)), since a successful oxygen sensor test reasonably implies calibration confirmation. To capture this, a suitable LTL property would be that whenever the self-test passes ('selfTestPass'), the oxygen sensor test must also pass ('testOxygenSensorPass'), i.e., self-test confirmation entails a passed calibration check of the oxygen sensor. This is best captured by a safety property using implication: globally, if 'selfTestPass' is true, then 'testOxygenSensorPass' must also be true. This uses template 30: G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3), where bool_exp1=selfTestPass and bool_exp3=testOxygenSensorPass. This ensures that under all circumstances, a self-test procedure passing means the oxygen sensor calibration is verified.

The requirement is that the self-test procedure must confirm the oxygen sensor is calibrated. In the atomic propositions, 'selfTestPass' indicates that all self-test steps have passed, and 'testOxygenSensorPass' indicates a successful oxygen sensor test (which can be understood as confirming calibration). Therefore, if the self-test passes, it must be the case that the oxygen sensor test has also passed, i.e., the oxygen sensor is confirmed calibrated. This maps to a safety property: whenever selfTestPass, it implies testOxygenSensorPass. The general form is G (selfTestPass -> testOxygenSensorPass).

The requirement is that the self-test procedure must confirm that the oxygen sensor is calibrated. In this context, 'selfTestPass' indicates that all self-test steps (which would include the oxygen sensor calibration) passed successfully, and 'testOxygenSensorPass' specifically indicates that the oxygen sensor test passed, i.e., the sensor is calibrated. Therefore, the most direct mapping of the requirement is that globally, if the overall self-test passes, then the oxygen sensor test must have passed as well. This is naturally formalized as a global implication: G(selfTestPass -> testOxygenSensorPass).

The requirement states that the self-test procedure needs to confirm the oxygen sensor is calibrated. In the available atomic propositions, 'selfTestPass' indicates that all self-test steps passed, and 'testOxygenSensorPass' indicates the oxygen sensor test passed (calibrated). Therefore, 'selfTestPass' should logically imply 'testOxygenSensorPass' whenever the self-test completes successfully, i.e., if the self-test ever passes, the oxygen sensor test must also pass. Template 30 (G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)) directly expresses this implication globally. Here, bool_exp1 = selfTestPass and bool_exp3 = testOxygenSensorPass. N_DURATION is not required for this template.

The requirement 'The self-test procedure shall confirm the oxygen sensor is calibrated.' implies that when the overall system self-test passes, it must include confirmation that the test for the oxygen sensor has also passed. The most direct mapping from the atomic propositions is to require that whenever 'selfTestPass' (all self-test steps passed successfully) holds, then 'testOxygenSensorPass' (oxygen sensor test pass) must also hold. This means passing the self-test is only valid if the oxygen sensor is calibrated (i.e., its test passes). This is captured by a safety property of the form: G (selfTestPass → testOxygenSensorPass).

The requirement is that the self-test procedure confirms the oxygen sensor is calibrated. In the atomic propositions, 'selfTestPass' indicates that all self-test steps (which would include checking the oxygen sensor calibration) have passed successfully. Additionally, 'testOxygenSensorPass' specifically indicates that the oxygen sensor test, i.e., its calibration, has passed. The LTL property should require that, globally, if the self-test passes, then the oxygen sensor test must also have passed. This is best represented by 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)', where 'bool_exp1' is 'selfTestPass' and 'bool_exp3' is 'testOxygenSensorPass'. This ensures that any time the self-test procedure reports success, the oxygen sensor calibration must be confirmed as passed as well.

The requirement 'The self-test procedure shall confirm the oxygen sensor is calibrated' corresponds to stating that whenever the self-test passes (selfTestPass), it should only happen if the oxygen sensor test passes (testOxygenSensorPass) as well. This means that a successful self-test implies a successful oxygen sensor test (which ensures the sensor is calibrated). Thus, we use the template 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)' (index 30), which reads as: Globally, whenever 'selfTestPass' occurs, then 'testOxygenSensorPass' must have occurred at the same time. This directly formalizes the intent that the oxygen sensor calibration must be confirmed as part of passing the self-test procedure.

The specification states that the self-test procedure should confirm that the oxygen sensor is calibrated. In context, it is reasonable to interpret 'the self-test procedure shall confirm the oxygen sensor is calibrated' as 'the system self-test must pass only if the oxygen sensor passes its test.' This is because the closest available atomic propositions are selfTestPass (all self-test steps passed successfully) and testOxygenSensorPass (oxygen sensor test pass (success)). Thus, a global property (G) is appropriate: whenever the self-test passes, it must be because the oxygen sensor test passes as well. This is best represented as 'always, if selfTestPass then testOxygenSensorPass.'

The requirement is that the self-test procedure should confirm proper calibration of the oxygen sensor. Within the given atomic propositions, 'selfTestPass' indicates all self-test steps passed successfully and 'testOxygenSensorPass' indicates the oxygen sensor test for calibration has passed. The most direct way to capture the requirement is: Whenever the self-test passes, it must be that the oxygen sensor test has also passed (i.e., has been confirmed calibrated). This is best expressed as a global implication: G(selfTestPass -> testOxygenSensorPass). This means at all times, if the overall self-test succeeds, then the oxygen sensor test (including its calibration) must have also succeeded.

The requirement states that the self-test procedure should confirm that the oxygen sensor is calibrated. Among the atomic propositions provided, 'selfTestPass' indicates all self-test steps passed successfully, which includes confirming oxygen sensor calibration, and 'testOxygenSensorPass' directly indicates the oxygen sensor test passed (calibrated). To capture the requirement, we model that globally, whenever the self-test as a whole passes, this should mean that the oxygen sensor test also passed (i.e., the sensor is calibrated). Therefore, we use an implication: whenever 'selfTestPass' holds, 'testOxygenSensorPass' must also hold. That is, it is never possible to pass the self-test without also confirming oxygen sensor calibration. This is best captured by the LTL template 30: G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3), with bool_exp1 = selfTestPass and bool_exp3 = testOxygenSensorPass. No other conditions are relevant, and N_DURATION is not needed for this template.
